title: American Kidney Fund Expands Kidney Health Coach Program with Educational Course on Lupus Nephritis
author: American Kidney Fund
description: The American Kidney Fund (AKF) has launched an online educational course on lupus nephritis through its Kidney Health Coach program to educate healthcare professionals and community members about symptoms, testing, diagnosis, and treatment. The course, developed with subject matter experts, aims to empower over 8,000 volunteer Kidney Health Coaches across all 50 states. An estimated 1.5 million Americans have lupus, with about half developing lupus nephritis, which disproportionately affects women and racial/ethnic minorities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: GSK is mentioned as a supporter of the lupus nephritis community presentation resource, indicating corporate partnership and sponsorship. However, the level of involvement and business impact is minimal and not detailed in the article.

title: Autoimmune Disease Therapeutics Market Size to Reach USD 137.85 Billion by 2035 as Biologics and Precision Medicine Drive Growth | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global autoimmune disease therapeutics market is projected to grow from USD 80.54 billion in 2025 to USD 137.85 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 5.52%. Growth is driven by rising prevalence of autoimmune diseases, adoption of biologics, JAK inhibitors, monoclonal antibodies, and precision medicine technologies. North America leads with 39.96% market share, while Asia-Pacific shows the fastest growth at 6.28% CAGR.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company is actively expanding its immunology pipeline with Phase III trials for next-generation JAK inhibitors targeting rheumatoid arthritis and psoriatic arthritis, demonstrating strong R&D investment in growing market segment.
Received FDA approval for new oral S1P receptor modulator for multiple sclerosis in 2024, strengthening its neurology portfolio and positioning it well in the fastest-growing indication segment.

Listed as leading market player in growing autoimmune therapeutics market with strong presence in biologics segment, which is expected to comprise 42% of U.S. autoimmune disease prescription revenues.
Major player in autoimmune therapeutics market positioned to benefit from growing demand for biologics and targeted therapies across multiple indications.
Leading market player with significant presence in autoimmune disease therapeutics, well-positioned to capture market growth driven by biologics adoption and precision medicine.

title: Moderna vs. Recursion: Which Cutting-Edge Pharma Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: Brendan Coffey
description: Moderna and Recursion Pharmaceuticals represent two distinct approaches to biotech innovation: Moderna leverages proven mRNA technology with diversifying pipeline into oncology and rare diseases, while Recursion uses AI-driven drug discovery. Despite both operating at losses, Moderna is favored for 2026 due to its established revenue base and proven formula, whereas Recursion remains in earlier development stages with revenue expected to decline due to lower milestone payments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Established revenue base of $1.9B, proven mRNA platform with 25 development candidates across 35 programs, strategic partnerships with Merck and Recordati, lower debt-to-equity ratio (0.2x), and analyst recommendation as the better buy for 2026 despite current losses.

Shows promise with AI-native discovery platform and partnerships with Roche, Genentech, and Sanofi, but remains clinical-stage with unproven technology, expected revenue decline of 11% in 2026, significant losses ($645M), and heavy reliance on partner funding and third-party infrastructure.
Strategic partner with Moderna for research and commercialization, but mentioned only in context of partnership without specific performance details.
Identified as competitive pressure on Moderna in the mRNA vaccine space, but no specific performance or investment thesis provided.
Strategic partner with both Recursion and competitive threat to Moderna, but mentioned only contextually without specific investment analysis.
Identified as competitive pressure on Moderna in vaccine market, but no specific performance details provided.

title: Big Pharma Has a Case of Merger Mania
author: Motley Fool Staff
description: The pharmaceutical industry is experiencing a record-breaking M&A wave in 2026, driven by looming patent cliffs worth $300 billion in annual revenue, FDA regulatory improvements for rare diseases, and the need to diversify portfolios. Companies are making strategic bolt-on acquisitions of late-stage drug candidates rather than massive mergers. While some firms like Eli Lilly are well-positioned, others like Merck and Pfizer face near-term patent expirations that could impact revenue significantly.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Described as the best-run big pharma currently, with strong GLP-1 portfolio (Tirzepatide/Mounjaro/Zepbound patented through 2036), robust pipeline, and strategic bolt-on acquisitions to diversify beyond current blockbusters. Favorable patent-cliff exposure compared to peers.

Aggressive M&A strategy with three major deals in 10 months, but faces significant risk as Keytruda (>50% of revenue) loses patent protection in 2028, leaving little room for error in integrating acquisitions.

Facing substantial patent cliffs ($17-18B in annual revenue losses) with Prevnar and cancer drugs expiring through 2027, but maintains strong dividend yield (~7%), affordable valuation, and oncology pipeline potential. Recovery expected by 2029.

Highlighted as a hidden gem that has easily beaten the market in recent years, with six FDA-approved treatments, robust pipeline, and strong entrepreneurial leadership focused on solving real patient problems.

Innovative transconjugation technology platform enabling less frequent dosing for endocrinology and oncology treatments. Positioned as potential acquisition target for big pharma seeking novel drug delivery technology.
Despite early GLP-1 monopoly with Ozempic, has significantly trailed the market over the past 10 years as competition from Eli Lilly and others intensified.
Actively participating in M&A wave with $10 billion immunology acquisition, indicating strategic portfolio diversification efforts.
Engaged in M&A activity with $10 billion acquisition, participating in industry consolidation trend.
Actively licensing drugs from other companies as part of portfolio diversification strategy during M&A wave.
Facing regulatory burden challenges that make clinical trial investments questionable, as noted by CEO regarding COVID-flu vaccine combination development.

title: Global HPV Vaccines Market to Grow Rapidly at a CAGR of ~8% by 2034 | DelveInsight
author: Delveinsight
description: The global HPV vaccines market is projected to grow from USD 8.9 billion in 2025 to USD 18.4 billion by 2034, driven by rising HPV-associated cancer prevalence, expanding government immunization programs, and advancements in multivalent vaccines. North America leads the market with 41% share, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a significant growth contributor. Key developments include Merck's presentation of long-term vaccine effectiveness data and China's approval of the first domestically-developed 9-valent HPV vaccine.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Merck is a leading player in the HPV vaccines market with Gardasil 9 and Gardasil products. The company is actively presenting clinical and real-world data demonstrating long-term vaccine effectiveness, reinforcing its market position and supporting continued demand for its vaccines.

Listed as a key company in the HPV vaccines market. Benefits from the overall market growth driven by expanding immunization programs and increasing awareness of HPV-related cancer prevention globally.
